question: I vanish the instant I’m measured, yet I mark the length of every life. What am I?
I cross a room without moving and open doors without a key. What am I?
Born from a mouth I have no tongue, carried by crowds I have no feet; I grow by subtraction. What am I?
hint: Spreads faster when details are removed.

question: Forward I guard your secrets, backward I reveal them. What word am I?
I’m counted on to be odd, but when I’m doubled I’m even trusted less. What am I?
I rest between two mirrors and make twins of strangers. What am I?
I begin where letters end and end where numbers start; without me maps are lost. What am I?
hint: Appears on both globes and music.

question: I’m always ahead yet never in front, always behind yet never at the back. What am I?
I’m worn to show you nothing, yet I’m full of meaning if you read me. What am I?
I split a house without breaking a wall and settle wars without a sword. What am I?
I’m a path drawn by a point that never moves. What am I?
I taste the world but never eat; too little and I’m dull, too much and I’m cruel. What am I?
You build me by taking away, and when I’m full I hold nothing. What am I?
I’m the first to arrive after the last goodbye, and the last to leave before the first hello. What am I?
I have a body of numbers, a face of letters, and hands that never point. What am I?
I’m a question no one asks until the answer is gone. What am I?
I keep time by standing still, and move fastest when I’m skipped. What am I?
I am a ladder laid flat that only climbs in minds. What am I?
Wear me and you’ll see less; close me and you’ll see more. What am I?
I draw a line no ruler can measure and erase it with a blink. What am I?
I’m the only guest who leaves before arriving. What am I?
I am a cage made of answers where a question is kept alive. What am I?
When I’m whole I’m invisible; when I’m broken I’m everywhere. What am I?
I’m a bridge whose arch you cannot walk, and crossing me makes old things new. What am I?
I’m borrowed by kings and beggars alike, but returned only by time. What am I?
hint: It doesn’t feel like a loan.

question: I begin with a promise and end with a price. What am I?
I am two rooms sharing one wall that never touch. What am I?
I am the shadow of a sound. What am I?
I’m a path that stands still, traveled by eyes but not by feet. What am I?
I’m a thief invited nightly who steals from tomorrow. What am I?
I travel on tongues, vanish in notes, and return in the margins. What am I?
I borrow your voice to disagree with you. What am I?
I am the only ladder that grows shorter as you climb. What am I?
I draw borders that no map can show, and end wars without a treaty. What am I?
I turn a river into a road and a road into a river. What am I?
I am the last tool a craftsman makes and the first a thief takes. What am I?
I am the echo that arrives before the shout. What am I?
I am the only mirror that works in the dark. What am I?
I make you lighter by adding weight. What am I?
I am the gate that swings on a single word. What am I?
I’m the house with one door that locks from inside only. What am I?
I’m the only debt repaid by spending more. What am I?
I am a promise with teeth. What am I?
I weigh nothing until I drop. What am I?
I am the first truth told by a liar. What am I?
I write without ink and speak without sound, yet I leave records that outlive stone. What am I?
I turn answers into questions and questions into roads. What am I?
I’m the silence that follows thunder and precedes rain. What am I?
I make twins of strangers by erasing a line. What am I?
I am the only chain that frees what it binds. What am I?
I move faster when you stop chasing me. What am I?
I’m the letter between two worlds that never touches either. What am I?
I can be broken by keeping me and kept by breaking me. What am I?
I multiply by being divided. What am I?
I keep you safe by letting you lose. What am I?
I am the lock that asks for its key. What am I?
I’m the copy that makes the original. What am I?
I hide in plain sight by showing everything. What am I?
I spend all day saving what I cannot spend. What am I?
I am the echo written before the sound. What am I?
I am the shadow of tomorrow on today. What am I?
I am the fire that cooks cold iron. What am I?
I’m the art of drawing without lines. What am I?
I am the sound you see and the picture you hear. What am I?
I stitch dawn to dusk with a single thread. What am I?
I empty my pockets by filling yours. What am I?
I turn questions into keys and doors into paths. What am I?
I’m the echo that arrives after the whisper. What am I?
I am the dessert you pay for before the meal. What am I?
I sharpen without touching and praise without speaking. What am I?
I’m the only light that grows darker when shared. What am I?
I am the roof that appears when you remove a floor. What am I?
I count what cannot be counted and miss what’s missing. What am I?
I am the road a prisoner paves to reach the open gate. What am I?
I taste of tomorrow and smell of yesterday. What am I?
I am the largest crowd one person can carry. What am I?
I’m the only window that closes by turning on. What am I?
I hold oceans without a drop of water. What am I?
I’m the page that writes the author. What am I?
I am the coin that buys nothing and changes everything. What am I?
I am the bridge whose toll is silence. What am I?
I am the question mark tattooed on a map. What am I?
I am the mirror that waits for you to turn around. What am I?
I am the letter that ends every conversation. What am I?
I am the blade that heals the tree. What am I?
I’m louder on paper than in a shout. What am I?
I am the debt you can only pay forward. What am I?
I’m the mask that shows your real face. What am I?
I am the rain that starts inside. What am I?
I am the seat that throws you forward to move you back. What am I?
I’m the only kind of silence that roars. What am I?
I am the light that makes stars invisible. What am I?
I am the calendar with only one square. What am I?
I borrow weight from distance. What am I?
I am the throne you must kneel to lift. What am I?
I am the road that ends in a circle. What am I?
I am the garden tended by storms. What am I?
I am the flame that drinks water. What am I?
I am the picture that draws the hand. What am I?
I am the promise that ends with a ring. What am I?
I am the coat that warms by thinning. What am I?
I am the current that flows uphill. What am I?
I am the bed that makes you tired to rest. What am I?
I am the whisper that stops a war. What am I?
I am the knot that holds by letting go. What am I?
I’m the green that grows in the dark. What am I?
I’m the word that shortens a sentence. What am I?
I am the house you can only enter after you leave. What am I?
I am the season made of seconds. What am I?
I am the password everyone knows but few can use. What am I?
I am the engine that runs on failure. What am I?
I am the ship that sails on streets. What am I?
I am the question that answers itself when you ask it. What am I?
I am the net that works best with holes. What am I?
I am the only chain that lengthens when broken. What am I?
I am the mirror that remembers. What am I?
I am the bell that rings inside. What am I?
I am the field where silence blooms. What am I?
I am the door that opens by closing. What am I?
I am the ladder built from holes. What am I?
I am the bank that pays in echoes. What am I?
I am the lock that opens when you break it. What am I?
I am the book whose ending writes the start. What am I?
I am the bridge built by listening. What am I?
I am the whisper that moves mountains. What am I?
I am the glass that’s strongest when cracked. What am I?
I am the stain that cleans. What am I?
